name: Tarrant County United Way 2-1-1
address: 1500 N Main St, Fort Worth, TX 76164
notes: Dial 2-1-1 (24/7) for connection to Tarrant County community resources.

name: Texas Workforce Commission - Choices Program
notes: Mandatory work component for TANF recipients in Texas. Administered locally through Workforce Solutions for Tarrant County.

name: Trinity Metro
address: 801 E Northside Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76102
notes: Bus, TEXRail, and ZIPZONE service across Tarrant County. Use the Trinity Metro app for real-time tracking and mobile passes. Limited Sunday service.

name: Salvation Army Fort Worth
address: 1855 E Lancaster Ave, Fort Worth, TX 76103
notes: Mabee Center campus on E Lancaster. Walk-in for food pantry. Shelter intake by appointment.

name: Tarrant Area Food Bank
notes: Mobile pantry schedule rotates across Tarrant County — call or check website for distribution times and locations.

name: GreenPath Financial Wellness - Fort Worth
eligibility: Open to all, free initial consultation
notes: HUD-approved nonprofit. Free credit report review. Debt management plans can reduce interest rates and consolidate payments.

name: Consumer Credit Counseling Service of Greater Dallas / Fort Worth
address: 8737 King George Dr, Dallas, TX 75235 (serves Fort Worth)
notes: Nonprofit. Sliding-scale fees. Phone and Fort Worth satellite appointments available. Free initial consultation.

name: Legal Aid of NorthWest Texas
address: 600 E Weatherford St, Fort Worth, TX 76102
notes: Free civil legal services for low-income residents of Tarrant County and 113 other NW Texas counties.
